phrases.text_local: L'entrée est bien gratuite pour tout le monde ?
phrases.pronunciation: lahn-TRAY eh byehn grah-TWEET poor too luh MOHND
phrases.cultural_note: The Musée Carnavalet has free permanent collection admission for all visitors — one of the best deals in Paris. Temporary exhibitions have a separate ticket. Located in two connected 16th-century mansions in Le Marais.

responses.english: Yes, admission is free. No ticket needed.
responses.phonetic: wee, lan-TRAY eh grah-TWEET. pah buh-ZWAHN duh bee-YAY
responses.french: Oui, l'entrée est gratuite. Pas besoin de billet.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the French Revolution rooms are
phrases.text_local: Où sont les salles sur la Révolution française ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo sohn lay sal soor lah ray-voh-LOO-syohn frahn-SEZ
phrases.cultural_note: The French Revolution rooms contain one of the world's richest collections of Revolution-era objects, including personal effects of Louis XVI and Marie Antoinette, guillotine-related artifacts, and revolutionary pamphlets.

responses.english: The Revolution rooms are on the first floor.
responses.phonetic: lay sal duh lah ray-voh-loo-SYOHN sohn toh pruh-MYAY ay-TAHZH
responses.french: Les salles de la Révolution sont au premier étage.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how long the visit usually takes
phrases.text_local: La visite dure combien de temps en moyenne ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah vee-ZEET door kohm-BYEHN duh tahn ahn mwah-YEN
phrases.cultural_note: The museum covers two mansions. Allow at least ninety minutes to see the highlights.

responses.english: Allow about an hour and a half.
responses.phonetic: kohn-TAY ahn-vee-ROHN oon uhr ay duh-MEE
responses.french: Comptez environ une heure et demie.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a map of both mansions
phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous me donner un plan des deux hôtels particuliers ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-RYAY voo muh doh-NAY uhn plahn day duh oh-TEL par-tee-koo-LYAY
phrases.cultural_note: The museum occupies two interconnected Renaissance mansions: the Hôtel Carnavalet (where Madame de Sévigné lived from 1677-1696) and the Hôtel Le Peletier de Saint-Fargeau. A map is essential to navigate the 100+ rooms.

responses.english: The maps are at the reception, at the museum entrance.
responses.phonetic: lay plahn sohn tah lah-KUH-ee, ah lan-TRAY doo moo-ZAY
responses.french: Les plans sont à l'accueil, à l'entrée du musée.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there is wheelchair access
phrases.text_local: Il y a un accès pour les personnes à mobilité réduite ?
phrases.pronunciation: eel ee ah uhn ahk-SEH poor lay pehr-SOHN ah moh-bee-lee-TAY ray-DWEET
phrases.cultural_note: Historic Parisian buildings vary in accessibility. Ask staff about elevators and adapted routes.

responses.english: Yes, there is an elevator. Ask at the reception.
responses.phonetic: wee, eel ee ah uhn ah-sahn-SUHR. duh-mahn-DAY ah lah-KUH-ee
responses.french: Oui, il y a un ascenseur. Demandez à l'accueil.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the garden courtyard is open to the public
phrases.text_local: La cour-jardin est-elle accessible au public ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah koor-zhar-DEHN eh-TEL ak-seh-SEE-bluh oh poo-BLEEK
phrases.cultural_note: The museum's courtyard garden is one of Le Marais's hidden pleasures and is free to enter. The courtyard features an equestrian statue of Louis XIV and is a peaceful escape from the busy streets outside.

responses.english: Yes, the courtyard and garden are open to the public.
responses.phonetic: wee, lah koor ay luh zhar-DAN sohn too-VEHR oh poo-BLEEK
responses.french: Oui, la cour et le jardin sont ouverts au public.
